message:
perf(validate): cache regex patterns with OnceLock and validate at compile time (bd-3hrk)

Previously, regex patterns in #[validate(regex = "...")] were compiled
on every validation call, causing ~10x performance overhead. Now:

1. Patterns are validated at macro expansion time - invalid patterns
   produce compile_error! instead of runtime panics
2. Valid patterns use OnceLock for lazy initialization - compiled once
   on first use, then cached for all subsequent validations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -13,6 +13,8 @@ proc-macro = true
 proc-macro2 = "1"
 quote = "1"
 syn = { version = "2", features = ["full", "parsing"] }
+# Used at compile time to validate regex patterns in #[validate(regex = "...")]
+regex = "1"
 
 [lints]
 workspace = true
